output: Implemented batch 3 / T3 by wiring modality-aware gesture ownership through the existing timeline stack: `TimelineEditor` now passes the shared interaction policy into marquee, clip drag, ruler, and canvas flows; `TimeRuler` is the dedicated scrub owner; empty-space touch drag stays pan-first unless marquee is explicitly allowed; and clip trim now delays pointer capture, `preventDefault()`, and `interactionStateRef.current.resize` until a real resize gesture starts. Verified with type-checking and the full `TimelineCanvas` and `useClipDrag` test modules, then checkpointed progress to `.megaplan/plans/create-a-concrete-mobile-and-20260410-0149/execution_batch_3.json`.

task_updates.executor_notes: Implemented modality-aware timeline arbitration without introducing a second mobile timeline or a second persistence gate. `TimelineEditor` now passes `deviceClass`, `inputModality`, `interactionMode`, `gestureOwner`, `setInputModalityFromPointerType`, and `setGestureOwner` into `useClipDrag`, `useMarqueeSelect`, `TimeRuler`, and `TimelineCanvas`. `TimeRuler` now claims the dedicated scrub zone through shared gesture ownership. `useMarqueeSelect` keeps empty-space touch drag pan-first unless tablet touch selection mode explicitly allows marquee. `useClipDrag` continues to set `interactionStateRef.current.drag` only after drag threshold. `TimelineCanvas` now expands touch trim handles in trim mode and defers resize pointer capture, `preventDefault()`, `pendingOpsRef`, and `interactionStateRef.current.resize` until the resize gesture crosses threshold, preserving `interactionStateRef` as the only save/poll deferral gate. Verified with `npm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false` and `npm exec vitest run src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.test.tsx`.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. Ruler scrub, empty-canvas marquee, clip drag, and trim now resolve ownership through the shared interaction policy using both baseline device class and per-event `pointerType`. `TimeRuler` owns scrub gestures as the dedicated ruler zone, touch empty-space interactions stay pan-first unless tablet touch select mode explicitly allows marquee, touch clip drag only arms in move mode, and trim claims `trim` ownership only after the resize threshold is crossed. Because touch-specific gating is resolved from `pointerType`, iPad mouse and trackpad still follow desktop behavior instead of inheriting iPad touch rules.
